findings: Average Packet Size feature shows strong class-conditioned structural variation with high class separation between BENIGN and DDoS classes across multiple local contexts.
Local context summaries indicate substantial variance ratio differences and unique value count disparities in packet length features, consistent with structural variation patterns.
Unresolved tension exists between features with near-constant values and those with strong class-conditioned variation, suggesting heterogeneous structural behavior within the feature group.
contradictions: No direct contradictions observed in the locally acquired evidence.
limitations: Variance ratios and distribution verifications for features e9-e12 beyond Average Packet Size were not completed due to step budget exhaustion.
